website_functionality.additional_functionality_details:
We are a small UX design and product strategy studio called "Northline Studio" based in Portland, OR, moving from a very basic Squarespace site to a more robust marketing site that can also support light client interaction.

In addition to the items checked above, we would like:

1. Project case study builder
- We want a custom post type for case studies with flexible content blocks (problem statement, process timeline, quotes, image galleries, embedded prototypes, metrics). Ideally, our team can mix and match these blocks per case study without needing a developer.
- Each case study should support tagging by industry (Fintech, Healthcare, Nonprofit, etc.), service type (UX Audit, Product Strategy, Design Systems), and project size so potential clients can filter.

2. Simple client portal (password-protected)
- We do not need a full-blown project management tool, but we would like a basic client area where we can share workshop materials, PDFs, and links to Figma boards or research summaries.
- Ideally, each client has a dedicated page or mini-area with a simple login (we’re fine if this is just a shared password per client rather than individual user accounts to keep scope manageable).

3. Testimonial management
- We’d like a way to manage testimonials with fields for client name, company, role, and project, plus an optional headshot.
- These testimonials should be reusable throughout the site (e.g., on the homepage, services pages, and relevant case studies) via a simple selection interface in the CMS.

4. Events and workshops listing
- We occasionally run paid and free workshops (remote and in person). We’d like an events listing section where we can add upcoming events with date, time, format (online/in person), price, and a link to register (usually via Eventbrite or Luma).
- Past events should automatically move into an archive view so visitors can see the types of sessions we run.

5. Lead qualification on enquiry forms
- For our main enquiry form, we’d like a few additional fields that help us qualify leads, such as approximate budget range, rough timeline, and whether they already have product-market fit.
- We’d like conditional logic so that, for example, if someone selects a very low budget range, they’re automatically shown a message suggesting our smaller "UX audit" offering or pointing them to free resources.

6. Lightweight resources hub
- Over time, we want to build a resources section that houses downloadable templates (e.g., workshop agendas, stakeholder interview guides) and long-form guides.
- This can probably be an extension of the document library/blog setup, but we’d like tagging and categorization ("Founders", "Product Managers", "Design Leaders") and the ability to gate certain downloads behind an email capture.

7. Performance and accessibility considerations
- We work in UX, so it’s important that the site is fast, accessible, and mobile-first. We’d like basic accessibility baked in (proper heading structure, color contrast, keyboard navigation, alt text fields required in the CMS for images, etc.).
- We don’t need anything fancy like custom animations everywhere; we’d rather prioritize clarity, readability, and page speed.

8. Analytics and conversion tracking
- We’d like event tracking for key actions (form submissions, newsletter signups, clicks on email addresses and phone numbers, clicks on outbound links to Calendly or Eventbrite).
- We also want the ability to set up simple goal funnels (e.g., homepage → services → enquiry form → thank-you page) so we can see where people drop off.

9. Multilingual readiness (future-proofing)
- Right now, all content will be in English only, but we’d like the content structure to support adding a second language (likely Spanish) in the future without rebuilding the site from scratch. This doesn’t need to be implemented on day one; we just want the architecture to keep this in mind.

10. Simple style guide page
- It would be helpful to have a hidden or non-indexed "style guide" page that shows the main typography styles, buttons, forms, and content blocks so our internal team understands how to use the system consistently.

If any of these items significantly increase scope or conflict with the chosen CMS, we’re open to prioritizing the essentials (marketing pages, case studies, blog, enquiry forms) for the initial launch and handling the more advanced client portal and resources hub as a phase two.

system_integrations.systems_our_new_website_needs_to_integra:
- HubSpot CRM for capturing form submissions and newsletter signups, ideally with tagging based on form type and field values (e.g., "Lead Type: Founder", "Service Interest: Design Systems").
- Mailchimp for email newsletter management (we already have an active list and a few basic automations for welcome sequences).
- Google Analytics 4 for general site analytics, plus Google Tag Manager so we can configure additional events without needing development every time.
- Calendly for scheduling intro calls and paid consultations (we’d like to embed booking widgets on the contact and "Work with us" pages).
- Eventbrite (or similar event platforms like Luma) for workshop registrations — this can be as simple as linking out, but if possible we’d like events on our site to pull in key details or at least be easy to sync.
- Figma and Miro are central to our work; we don’t need deep integrations, but we’d like to be able to embed public prototypes or boards in case studies or resource articles.
- Social platforms: LinkedIn and Instagram primarily — we’d like social share buttons on blog posts and case studies, and optionally a simple Instagram feed on the homepage (not critical if it hurts performance).

If any of these integrations are better handled as phase two or via no-code connectors like Zapier or Make, we’re open to your recommendations.
